superseded_reason: Spike-001 (3cee12a) proved this file's (upstream_avg_perm, upstream_avg_ctr_bins_avg_order) pair is INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT under the upstream algorithm: the bins are stored in the CTR materialization order Q (= storage-reorder S o LearnPermutation), but were paired here with upstream_avg_perm=GetLearnPermutationArray()=[11,18,15,29,..] (the leaf-index iteration order, a DIFFERENT order), and LearnTargetClass was never captured. Re-instrumentation (plan 05-18, 2026-06-15) captured the self-consistent (Q, target=LearnTargetClass[1]=binarized-y, bins) tuple atomically in live_trainer_self_consistent.json. DO NOT re-pin against this file. Kept for the Spike-001 evidence trail.
scenario: tensor_ctr_e2e config family, pc=4, N=30, seed=0, thread_count=1
authorization: 2026-06-15 CONTEXT decision revision (live-trainer instrumentation, bar (c) toolchain-provision path)
purpose: Localize the bar-(c) blocker AFTER structure-fold cycling + corrected averaging permutation were ported. The remaining gap is the AveragingFold ONLINE-CTR ui8 bin VALUES (ComputeOnlineCTRs(AveragingFold)), which cb_train::materialize_ctr_feature does not reproduce even with the bit-exact averaging permutation.
findings: Structure-fold cycling RECOVERED: takenFold = Folds[Rand.GenRand() % foldCount] per iteration (train.cpp:208); pc=4 sequence [0,2,0,2,2]; leaf values stay on fixed AveragingFold (approx_calcer.cpp:1082).

Averaging permutation was WRONG in create_folds: the prior 'averaging shuffle starts at callcount==learning_folds' rule gave [23,19,25,...] which only COINCIDENTALLY produced the partition COUNTS [6,0,10,14]/[6,0,7,17]. The TRUE upstream averaging permutation (instrumented fold_cc GetCallCount): shuffle starts at cc=29 (pc=1) / cc=87 (pc=4) = learning_folds full Fisher-Yates passes. Our uniform-shuffle advanced to cc=87 reproduces upstream [11,18,15,29,16,...] BIT-EXACT.

Correcting create_folds to the true permutation makes pc=4 tree0 [6,0,10,14] AND keeps the pc=4 partition oracle green, BUT regresses pc=1/pc=2 partition [6,0,7,17] -> [6,0,8,16] and the tensor_ctr_e2e (pc=1) e2e — because those locks were pinned to the OLD wrong-permutation partition that the WRONG online-CTR-bins happened to produce.

ROOT REMAINING BLOCKER (newly localized): our online-CTR ui8 bins differ from upstream's ComputeOnlineCTRs(AveragingFold) even with the correct permutation. Upstream avg-order bins [7,7,7,11,3,7,2,11,7,12,9,...] are NOT reproduced by materialize_ctr_feature's online-prefix (single-cat Borders, prior 0.5, border_count 15): first 4 match (7,7,7,11) then diverge at pos 4 (ours 7, upstream 3). Neither class-0-as-good nor the {0,1} combination projection reproduces the sequence. The averaging-fold online CTR is recomputed per the LIVE training-time path and its exact bin values are a property of ComputeOnlineCTRs not captured by the offline materialization.
recommendation: bar (c) requires a cb_train online-CTR materialization fix to match ComputeOnlineCTRs(AveragingFold) bit-exact (a CTR-subsystem change with blast radius across ALL CTR oracles, since the current pc=1/pc=2/pc=4 partition locks are pinned to the compensating wrong-permutation+wrong-bins combination). This is an architectural CTR-parity task beyond porting the structure-fold rule. Finalize on bars (a),(b),(d),(e); defer (c) with this localized evidence.
